title: Sweet Malaysian Oreo Cream Bao
description: A sweet and aerated bao filled with Oreo cream and a hint of Malaysian spices, perfect for kids and FODMAP-friendly.

ingredients.name: Warm Oreo cream
ingredients.notes: from 24 Oreo cookies, crushed and blended with a little water

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine rice flour, sugar, yeast, and salt. Mix well until combined.
Gradually add in warm water to the dry ingredients and mix until a smooth, sticky dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes elastic and smooth.
Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rest in a warm place for 1 hour, or until it has doubled in size.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 6 equal pieces.
Roll each piece into a ball and flatten it slightly into a disk shape.
Place a spoonful of warm Oreo cream in the center of each disk, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Dampen the edges with water and fold the dough over the filling, pressing the edges together to seal the bao.
Place the bao on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, brush with egg wash, and bake for 15 minutes, or until golden brown.
Let the bao cool on a wire rack for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve warm, drizzled with coconut milk if desired.
